rulings.Case Title: FREDERICK HUTCHINS MOLL VS. JUDITH M BRASS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT JUDITH BRASS' OBJECTIONS TO PROPOSED JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 19, 2025 line 1. DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT JUDITH BRASS' OBJECTIONS TO PROPOSED JUDGMENT is continued to February 26, 2025. No later than February 21, 2025 parties to deliver courtesy copies of the proposed judgment and objections thereto with cover letters reflecting new hearing date. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC VS. ROBERT BOWERS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 19, 2025 line 15. DEFENDANT ROBERT BOWERS DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. Plaintiff does not allege that SFRO is applicable to defendants tenancy and attaches exhibits containing facts supporting the lack of allegation of SFRO's applicability. The Court also notes that according to the Notice of the Demurrer, the basis of the demurrer is missing allegations in the "Notice." This argument is not supported by any authorities in the body of the Points and Authorities, instead Defendant argues that allegations are missing in the Complaint (vs. Notice). =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
